paper: TVC Study 11-Year Follow-Up (Verma-Fuehring et al., BMC Ophthalmology 2025)

key_improvements: 100% numerical accuracy (up from systematic errors in Iter1)
No fabricated references (strict adherence to Iter1 rule)
Instrument names and measurement protocols included (Goldmann, Spectralis, Octopus 900)
Discussion has 6+ thematic paragraphs with strengths section

top_3_strengths: Perfect numerical accuracy: 34/34 verified data points match the source exactly, a dramatic improvement from Iteration 1
Comprehensive Results section covering all outcome domains (success rates, IOP, medications, visual, structural, complications, revisions) with appropriate statistical detail
Well-structured Discussion with 6 thematic paragraphs plus separate Limitations, following guidelines for depth and organization
top_3_weaknesses: Incomplete reference list (only 1 of ~20-30 citations available) limits the scholarly context and Discussion breadth
Missing baseline demographics (age, sex distribution) that were likely in the original Table 1 but could not be extracted from web sources
Discussion lacks comparison with international surgical series beyond the original TVC 2-year data and general literature complication ranges
